skill.name: React Native Mobile Development
skill.description: Guides React Native and Expo mobile app setup, component development, platform-specific implementation, and common troubleshooting workflows.
skill.summary: Practical guidance for building React Native and Expo mobile apps.
skill.author: BabakBar

security_audit.summary: All 20 static detections are false positives caused by Markdown fences, inline code, documentation links, and ordinary mobile development instructions. The skill contains no prompt-injection language, data-exfiltration intent, hidden commands, or system reconnaissance.

static_findings.snippet: 1. **Performance**: Use `StyleSheet.create()`, avoid inline styles, optimize images
- **Metro won't start**: Clear cache with `npx expo start --clear`
npm run android        # Run on Android Emulator

content.user_title: Build React Native Apps with Expo
content.value_statement: Mobile projects need clear guidance for setup, platform differences, and common interface patterns. This skill provides focused React Native and Expo workflows for building and troubleshooting mobile apps.
actual_capabilities: Explains npm and Expo commands for starting, building, and prebuilding a mobile project.
Provides a reusable React Native component structure with styles and press handling.
Shows platform-specific rendering, values, and styles for iOS and Android.
Demonstrates list, form, and loading-state patterns using React Native components.
Lists practical checks for Metro, native module, build, and simulator problems.
limitations: It does not create, run, or test a project without user approval and a configured environment.
It does not replace platform documentation for native build signing or store submission.
Examples are general patterns and require adaptation to the project architecture.
It does not diagnose device-specific issues without logs and project context.

use_cases.title: Start an Expo project
use_cases.description: Set up a development workflow and choose the appropriate Expo commands for a new mobile application.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developer new to mobile development

use_cases.title: Build cross-platform components
use_cases.description: Create accessible React Native components that adapt layout and behavior for iOS and Android.
use_cases.target_user: React developer building a mobile interface

use_cases.title: Resolve local development issues
use_cases.description: Work through common Metro, prebuild, configuration, and simulator issues with targeted checks.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile developer maintaining an Expo app

prompt_templates.title: Create a starter component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a React Native button component with a title, press handler, accessible label, and StyleSheet styles.

prompt_templates.title: Add platform-specific styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to apply iOS shadow styles and Android elevation to this React Native card component.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a long list
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this screen design and propose a FlatList structure with stable keys, empty state, and loading state.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Expo troubleshooting
prompt_templates.prompt: My Expo build fails after adding a native module. Create a step-by-step diagnostic plan using my app configuration and build logs.

output_examples.input: I need a tappable React Native settings row.
output: A component outline with a label and press callback.
Accessibility labels and roles for the touch target.

output_examples.input: My Expo project reports a native module error.
output: A short diagnostic sequence for dependency compatibility.
A recommendation to regenerate native code when appropriate.
A request for the exact error and app configuration if the problem remains.

best_practices: Use StyleSheet.create and test layouts on both iOS and Android.
Add accessibility labels and roles to interactive controls.
Review package compatibility before installing dependencies or regenerating native projects.
anti_patterns: Do not assume iOS and Android use identical layout or native behavior.
Do not use array indexes as list keys when stable item identifiers exist.
Do not run clean or prebuild commands before checking uncommitted project changes.

faq.question: What does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers React Native and Expo setup, component patterns, platform-specific code, and common troubleshooting steps.

faq.question: Does it support both iOS and Android?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for platform-specific rendering, values, and styles on iOS and Android.

faq.question: Can it help with Expo commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents common Expo commands for starting projects, installing compatible packages, and generating native code.

faq.question: Can it fix a failed build automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest diagnostic steps, but build fixes require project details, logs, and user-approved actions.

faq.question: Does it provide native module guidance?
faq.answer: It identifies basic prebuild and configuration checks, but complex native integration needs platform-specific documentation.

faq.question: What information helps with troubleshooting?
faq.answer: Provide the exact error, command used, Expo and React Native versions, platform, and relevant configuration files.
